title: wedelia jacquinii subsp. parviflora
intro: Wedelia jacquinii subsp. parviflora, also known as the small-flowered Wedelia or yellow flower, is a subspecies of plant belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Wedelia jacquinii subsp. parviflora include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to alleviate various health conditions.
